items.title: Bank Rakyat hit with RM1mil fine over cybersecurity breach
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia has imposed an administrative penalty of RM1 million on Bank Kerjasama Rakyat Malaysia Bhd (Bank Rakyat) for cybersecurity and customer information breaches occurred on Jan 20. The central bank said Bank Rakyat had failed to implement robust cybersecurity standards as required under the risk management in technology policy document (RMiT PD). Bank Rakyat also failed to safeguard customer information through adequate controls as required under the management of customer information and permitted disclosures policy document (MCIPD PD).

items.title: Experts: Better SPM results do not prove exam reforms worked
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The improved performance of the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) cohort should be interpreted with nuance and not reduced to simplistic conclusions, experts said. Educationist and Skolar Malaysia co-founder Dr Tazli Azizan said the improvement should be viewed in context, adding that student outcomes cannot be attributed to a single factor such as the omission of UPSR and PT3. Tazli said school-based assessment and continuous support can play a role in student performance when implemented meaningfully and consistently.

items.title: SUPP president: Lily Yong’s sacking under legal process
items.summary: KUCHING, April 1 — The sacking of Sarawak United People’s Party (SUPP) Kuching chairman Datuk Lily Yong Lee Lee is currently under a “legal process”, said Datuk Amar Dr Sim Kui Hian. SUPP’s leadership has yet to address Yong’s expulsion last month along with branch secretary Voong Nam Jing. On March 26, SUPP Kuching Youth chief Nicholas Wung confirmed that the party’s Central Working Committee (CWC) had sacked the two leaders.

items.title: Account service provider held by MACC for false claims
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has remanded an account service provider from a business management consultancy firm for allegedly submitting documents containing false claims amounting to about RM4 million. A four-day remand order, until April 4, 2026, was issued by magistrate Ezrene Zakariah following an application by MACC at the Putrajaya magistrate's court this morning. "He is suspected of submitting documents containing false particulars involving various claims, including travel and transportation; accounting fees; purchase of generators and diesel; credit card payments; entertainment claims; expenses related to property, plant and equipment (PPE); petty cash claims; unidentified expenditure; payments for landscaping works; and salary payments," the source said.

items.title: Fahmi says stricter penalties for fatal drunk driving not yet discussed, prosecutions ongoing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said that decisions to prosecute individuals suspected of breaking the law, including cases involving drunk drivers, fall under the authority of the Attorney General, with investigations guided by existing legal frameworks and evidence. He said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) conduct inquiries not only under road transport laws but also on broader legal grounds when sufficient evidence exists to justify prosecution. "The matter will proceed according to the Attorney General’s office regarding legal aspects,” he said during the ministry's weekly press conference here today.

items.title: Raided: Store making customers buy other groceries before getting subsidised cooking oil
items.summary: TAPAH: A retail premises here has come under scrutiny after enforcement officers uncovered a tactic forcing customers to purchase additional groceries before being allowed to buy subsidised cooking oil. The operation, conducted yesterday at 10.30am by the Perak Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry enforcement officers, followed a public complaint alleging "conditional sales" involving 1kg packet cooking oil. State ministry director, Datuk Kamalludin Ismail, said enforcement officers deployed undercover methods, including a test purchase, to verify the claim.

items.title: MOE denies viral statement on SPM 2025 achievement by race, says deputy minister
items.summary: DENGKIL, April 1 — A viral press statement displaying the percentage of student achievement by race for the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) results is untrue, said Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh. He said that the Ministry of Education (MOE) did not issue the statement, and the information was not part of the official results announcement made yesterday. Wong said the conduct of the SPM examination and the announcement of results were carried out in accordance with established benchmarks and standards, without any compromise.

items.title: PM pays tribute to RTD on 80th anniversary, urges to enhance service quality
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today paid tribute to the Road Transport Department (RTD) on its 80th anniversary, and called on the agency to further strengthen enforcement and enhance its service quality to maintain public confidence in the agency. In a Facebook post, Anwar said increasingly complex road conditions demand continued firmness, accountability and efficiency from the department. "Happy 80th anniversary to RTD.

items.title: PM Anwar to meet state leaders Thursday over global energy crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is set to meet all Chief Ministers and Menteris Besar tomorrow to discuss the ongoing global energy crisis and its implications for Malaysia’s economy. “God willing, there will be several press conferences in the afternoon led by Deputy Prime Minister II Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, and potentially statements from the prime minister himself following the meeting,” he said at the ministry’s weekly press conference today. The prime minister, who also serves as Finance Minister I, said the National Economic Action Council (NEAC) had held in-depth discussions on the deteriorating global energy situation and its possible impact on the country’s economy and the welfare of the rakyat.

items.title: Thunderstorms in multiple states until 9pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (METMalaysia) has issued a warning for thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds across several states until 9pm. The alert covers, among others, Kedah (Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Yan, Pendang, Baling, Kulim, and Bandar Baharu); Penang (Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah, and Seberang Perai Selatan) and Perak. Other affected states are Kelantan (Gua Musang); Pahang (Tanah Tinggi Cameron, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan, and Bera); Selangor (Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat, and Sepang); Negeri Sembilan, Melaka, Johor (Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Pontian, Kulai, and Johor Baru); Sarawak (including Kuching, Bau, Serian, Samarahan, Sri Aman, Betong, Sarikei, Sibu, Kapit, Bintulu, Miri, and Limbang); and Sabah (Pedalaman, Tawau, and Sandakan).

items.title: SJKT Dengkil redevelopment ahead of schedule, completion expected early, says deputy education minister
items.summary: DENGKIL, April 1 — The redevelopment project for dilapidated buildings at Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan Tamil (SJKT) Dengkil here is progressing smoothly and is ahead of schedule, with completion expected earlier than originally planned, said Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh. Wong said the project’s physical progress has reached 65.14 per cent compared to the original target of about 50 per cent in March, placing it 15.14 per cent ahead of schedule and approximately 70 days ahead of the contract timeline. The new block that houses pre-school students is almost complete as part of the Replacement Building Project (Daif 2022) at the Dengkil Tamil National Type School April 1, 2026.

items.title: CEO: Energy Commission reviews legal, regulatory frameworks to support sector stability, align with energy needs
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 1 — The Energy Commission (ST) is reviewing several legal and regulatory frameworks, including mechanisms for electricity cross-border trading, as part of its efforts to support energy sector stability and sustainable growth. Its chief executive officer, Siti Safinah Salleh, said the commission is currently assessing existing policies while developing new frameworks to ensure the sector remains aligned with future requirements. “We are currently reviewing and also developing several legal and regulatory frameworks to ensure that the sector remains aligned with future requirements and policies,” she said at the ST Annual Regulatory Review (STARR) 2026 here today.

items.title: Explosions rock Teheran as Trump signals rapid end to US-Israel war with Iran
items.summary: TEHERAN: Teheran was rocked by a fresh wave of explosions Wednesday, as US President Donald Trump said the war with Iran could be over in two or three weeks. But Trump, whose statements on the war have swung from combative to conciliatory, said late Tuesday that the fighting could be over in "two weeks, maybe three." The White House said he would give "an important update on Iran" to the nation at 9pm Wednesday (0100 GMT Thursday).

items.title: Petronas operations stable, weekly fuel prices to be announced every Wednesday, says comms minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said that Petronas operations remain stable for the period between April and May, following a detailed report presented at yesterday’s National Economic Action Council (NEAC) meeting, where several aspects of the company’s operations were carefully reviewed. He also said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has directed that the situation continue to be closely monitored. “For the period between April and May, Petronas operations remain under control.

items.title: Johor aims to lead in FDI, strengthens Johor‑Singapore SEZ framework, says Hasni
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 1 — Johor is committed to sustaining its position as a leading investment destination and to remain at the forefront of foreign direct investment (FDI) inflows, which reached RM110.1 billion in 2025, the highest among all states. Johor State Economic and Investment Advisor Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad said the state will not remain complacent despite its strong performance, but will enhance its competitiveness and investment ecosystem continuously. He also said the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) will serve as a key blueprint to provide clarity and certainty for both new and existing investors, while also enabling the state to retain and expand current investments.

items.title: Penang revises quit rent mechanism to ease landowner burden
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang government has introduced improvements to its quit rent rates mechanism following concerns since its implementation earlier this year. This applies to First Grade land, Conditions A, B, C, as well as Nil Category (NIL) land without planning permission. He said under the new approach, quit rent rates would be aligned with the Building (Others) category, set at RM0.70 per square metre for urban land and RM0.50 for rural land, significantly lower than the previously proposed RM3.25.

items.title: Police credit Volunteer Smartphone Patrol app for crime drop during Hari Raya
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The Volunteer Smartphone Patrol (VSP) application used during Op Selamat 26 has helped lower the crime index during this year’s Hari Raya Aidilfitri festive period. Bukit Aman Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department (JPJKK) director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri said the crime index fell by 111 cases, from 858 cases during Op Selamat 24 (March 29 to April 3, 2025) to 747 cases during Op Selamat 26 (March 19 to 24, 2026). Mohd Yusri said police visited 61,122 vacant houses and carried out 31,636 Mobile Patrol Vehicle (MPV) rounds during Op Selamat 26.

items.title: Socso set to enforce Gig Workers Act, urges platform providers to integrate systems for contributions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The Social Security Organisation (PERKESO) is fully prepared to implement social protection for gig workers, including through system integration using Application Programming Interface (API) technology with platform providers. “This step is in line with the implementation of the Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872), which mandates all platform providers to register their gig workers with PERKESO. “This approach not only expands social security coverage but also ensures that gig workers receive appropriate protection without compromising their work flexibility,” the agency said.

items.title: Authorities say Monet painting linked to 1MDB sold, funds to return to Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Malaysian authorities have confirmed that a Claude Monet painting linked to the 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) scandal has been sold for €25.22 million (about RM118 million). According to the New Straits Times (NST), Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Special Operations Division senior director Datuk Mohamad Zamri Zainul Abidin said the artwork and its proceeds have been identified and will be returned to the government. NST also reported that sources indicated the piece is believed to be part of Monet’s Water Lilies series, titled Nymphéas avec Reflets de Hautes Herbes .

items.title: Segamat Hospital emergency dept stretched thin with 13 doctors on round-the-clock duty
items.summary: SEGAMAT: Segamat Hospital's emergency and trauma department is facing a critical manpower crunch, with just 13 medical officers (MOs) covering 24-hour emergency services for the district. CodeBlue is an independent Malaysian health policy and public health journalism platform. In response, the Johor State Health Department acknowledged the imbalance of MO distribution between health clinics and Segamat Hospital.

items.title: Driver dies, 2 injured in Sungai Besar car accident
items.summary: KUALA SELANGOR: A car driver was killed and two others injured after the vehicle they were travelling in skidded, crashed into a road divider, and overturned at Kampung Belia 2, Sungai Panjang, Sungai Besar, today. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said they received a call about the incident at 1.25pm. "A team of seven personnel, along with a fire engine from the Sungai Besar Fire and Rescue Station, was dispatched to the scene.

items.title: Energy Commission to ensure supply of electricity generation feedstock despite global energy pressures, says CEO
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 1 — The Energy Commission (ST) has taken steps to ensure the continued supply of feedstock, particularly gas and coal for electricity generation, as it monitors developments in West Asia with the conflict in the region entering its second month. She said cost implications would kick in as soon as June. On the Automatic Fuel Adjustment (AFA), Siti Safinah said, despite the ongoing conflicts, the AFA adjustment is projected to continue reflecting rebates up until June or July onwards.

items.title: New communications command centre set up to coordinate economic measures
items.summary: Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the cabinet had agreed to set up a Communications Command Centre under the National Economic Action Council. The centre, chaired by Deputy Prime Minister II Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, will coordinate the government's messaging on economic responses and measures in light of the conflict. In addition, Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan and Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohamad Nasir will meet the Parliamentary Special Select Committee on Finance and Economy to present the government's strategies and initiatives to date, particularly regarding economic and financial challenges arising from the conflict.

items.title: Bukit Aman examines phone linked to loan shark syndicate
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A mobile phone allegedly belonging to a member of a loan shark syndicate has been handed over to Bukit Aman's forensic unit for analysis. Brickfields police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said authorities are currently awaiting the outcome of the analysis from the relevant department. #KW: Brickfields police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook, mobile phone, Bukit Aman, loan shark, Malaysian Humanitarian Organisation, MHO, secretary-general, Datuk Hishamuddin Hashim © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Malaysia enforces foreign card ban on RON95 fuel at self‑service terminals nationwide, effective today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The restriction on the purchase of RON95 petrol using foreign credit and debit cards at self-service terminals at petrol stations is being enforced in stages nationwide from today. Checks in several states, particularly those bordering neighbouring countries, found that the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) is actively carrying out enforcement and monitoring operations at petrol stations. Petrol stations appear well-prepared and are complying with regulations governing the use of foreign credit and debit cards for RON95 purchases at self-service terminals.

items.title: Domestic Trade Ministry: Fuel supply stable nationwide, tighter checks in Sabah, Sarawak, Labuan
items.summary: BAU, April 1 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) has ensured that fuel supply remains sufficient to meet public demand through an effective nationwide daily monitoring system, and will continue to strengthen enforcement in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan, said director-general Datuk Azman Adam. He said there have been no fuel shortages so far despite increased demand at several petrol stations following the recent festive season. He said enforcement in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan is necessary to strengthen control over fuel supply, particularly diesel, in order to ensure supply continuity, prevent disruptions, and curb any misappropriation in the market.

items.title: ‘A step towards truth’: Susanna Liew hails decision allowing access to special task force report
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Susanna Liew, the wife of missing Pastor Raymond Koh, has welcomed the government’s decision to withdraw its appeal and allow her access to the Special Task Force Report (STF Report) on his abduction. She said the move finally gives her the same treatment as Norhayati Mohd Ariffin, the wife of Amri Che Mat, who had earlier been granted access to the report. “We thank the government for treating me and Pastor Raymond Koh equally before the law.

items.title: Mother informs teacher about child's encounter with Zara Qairina
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A mother informed a school teacher about her child's encounter with the late Zara Qairina Mahathir to seek clarification on the inquest. The 66th witness told the Coroner's Court that she only learned between September and October last year that her eldest child could have been the last person to interact with Zara on July 16. Nurul Rafeeqa is representing Zara's father.

items.title: Energy Commission: New incentive leaves 85pc of households’ electricity bills unchanged
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 1 — The Energy Commission (ST) has assured that about 85 per cent of domestic households will experience little impact on their electricity bills following the implementation of the Energy Efficiency Incentive, amid the current global energy crisis. It is insulated from the energy markets, global energy markets. She reminded consumers to use energy efficiently amid the current global energy crisis.

items.title: United Malacca set to benefit from higher CPO prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: United Malacca Bhd is set to benefit from rising crude palm oil (CPO) prices, supported by strong growth in fresh fruit bunch (FFB) output, according to CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd. The company's 9MFY26 core net profit rose 51.2 per cent YoY to RM128.6 million, surpassing CIMB Securities' forecast at 126 per cent and reaching 97 per cent of the full-year consensus estimate. EBITDA from Malaysian plantations rose 31.1 per cent YoY to RM172.7 million, supported by a 19.6 per cent YoY increase in FFB production and a 16.2 per cent YoY rise in palm kernel prices to RM3,326 per tonne.

items.title: 'We still live in fear': RCI push renewed a year after gas explosion
items.summary: SUBANG JAYA: Environmental watchdogs and residents of Putra Heights — the neighbourhood devastated by a massive gas explosion from an underground pipeline on April 1 last year — have renewed calls for a Royal Commission of Inquiry. The groups and residents said the call was not meant to assign blame, but to uncover the truth and raise awareness of potential future risks involving the 2,600km Peninsular Gas Utilisation (PGU) pipeline network. Rimbawatch director Adam Farhan said calls for an RCI had been made since the first day of the explosion and remained unanswered a year on.

items.title: Durian seller remanded for peeping on underage cousin while she bathed
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A durian seller has been remanded for three days by the Magistrate's Court here today for allegedly peeping on his female cousin while she was bathing at a house in Parit Sulong yesterday. The remand order against the 37-year-old suspect, a father of one, was issued by Magistrate Nurasidah A Rahman, effective from today until Friday. The victim later told her 39-year-old mother that she saw a man peeping before the suspect fled the scene.

items.title: [UPDATED] MACC probe into Azam Baki's shares, 'corporate mafia' will be made public – Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: KUALA LUMPUR: The investigation into Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki's shareholdings and allegations of a so-called "corporate mafia" within the commission will be made public. Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil assured that disclosure would take place at the appropriate time. Fahmi said that Putrajaya must first await input from the Chief Secretary to the Government and allow the proper procedures to be completed, noting that no findings from the investigation have yet been submitted to the cabinet.

items.title: Fisherman’s body found after falling from boat in Hutan Melintang
items.summary: IPOH, April 1 — The body of a fisherman who drowned after falling off his boat while trying to check on his propeller at Lot 125B (Pelantar 10) Hutan Melintang, near Bagan Datuk yesterday, has been found. Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant director (Operations) Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the body of Pee Kian Kok, 55, was found about 10 metres from the scene after the search and rescue team resumed the operations this morning using the surface searching method. The Bagan Datuk district Civil Defence Force (CDF) operations control centre said a team was mobilised to the scene for observation, but no search was conducted due to weather factors.

items.title: UDA names former UMW Holdings top executive as new CEO
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: UDA Holdings Bhd has appointed Datuk Seri Ahmad Fuaad Kenali as chief executive officer effective from today. Previously, Fuaad held several leadership positions including UMW Holdings Bhd group chief executive officer, Malakoff Corp Bhd chief executive officer, Proton Holdings Bhd chief executive officer as well as Astro Malaysia Holdings Bhd chief financial officer. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Maybank explores collaboration with Media Prima
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malayan Banking Bhd (Maybank) is exploring potential areas of collaboration with Media Prima Bhd, particularly in digital services, following a visit to the media group's headquarters at Balai Berita in Bangsar here. Khairussaleh, accompanied by group chief financial officer Shafiq Abdul Jabbar, was received by Media Prima group managing director Datuk Mohd Rafiq Mat Razali. Also present were group chief financial officer Rosli Sabarudin; Media Prima news and current affairs division group managing editor Jasbant Singh; and deputy group managing editor of news and current affairs and New Straits Times group editor Farrah Naz Karim.

items.title: Customs: Absolute ban on e‑waste imports into Malaysia takes effect today
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 1 — The importation of electronic waste (e-waste) into Malaysia has officially been gazetted as an absolutely prohibited item under the First Schedule of the Customs (Prohibition on Imports) Order 2023. This follows the Federal Government Gazette of the Customs (Prohibition on Imports) Order (Amendment) (No. Royal Malaysian Customs Department (JKDM) Director-General Datuk Amran Ahmad said the move aligns with decisions made during the Special Task Force Meeting on the Direction of Management Enforcement of Plastic Waste and E-Waste Importation No.

items.title: Perodua backs clarity on NAP as ministry defends EV policy stance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Perusahaan Otomobil Kedua Sdn Bhd (Perodua) has backed Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry's clarification on the National Automotive Policy (NAP), underscoring its role in attracting investments and strengthening Malaysia's economic base. The national carmaker described the current guidelines as a crucial step towards building a more resilient automotive ecosystem, delivering broad-based gains across the industry. "The policy, vision and roadmap set out by Miti provide a comprehensive, transparent and consistent guide for automotive industry players," Perodua said in a statement.

items.title: Woman lured by luxury admits role in RM3.4mil theft
items.summary: BENTONG: Driven by the lure of luxury and wealth, a young woman and her husband carried out a daring RM3.4 million heist targeting a prominent entrepreneur. "Driven by an overwhelming desire for luxury, I took part in a RM3.4 million heist involving cash and jewellery belonging to a well-known female entrepreneur," said 24-year-old convict Ayu (not her real name). She said the crime, orchestrated by her brother-in-law, sister and husband, took place two years ago.

items.title: Anwar pays tribute to JPJ on 80th anniversary, urges stronger enforcement and service quality
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today paid tribute to the Road Transport Department (JPJ) on its 80th anniversary, and called on the agency to further strengthen enforcement and enhance its service quality to maintain public confidence in the agency. In a Facebook post, Anwar said increasingly complex road conditions demand continued firmness, accountability and efficiency from the department. “Happy 80th anniversary to JPJ.

items.title: Penang lowers quit rent for old industrial plots after landowners protest steep hikes
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 1 — Penang has moved to ease the burden on certain landowners by reducing annual quit rent charges after complaints over steep increases under the state’s revised rates. According to Free Malaysia Today (FMT), the state executive council has agreed to lower charges for older land titles used for business and industrial purposes, particularly those without planning approval. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said such plots often involved temporary structures, depots and lorry sites, with more than 800 cases identified.

items.title: RM128.66mil Monet sale boosts 1MDB asset recovery
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The sale of a high-value painting by French master Claude Monet for 25.22 million euros (about RM128.66 million) has strengthened Malaysia's ongoing efforts to recover assets linked to the 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) scandal. Sources said the painting is believed to be part of Monet's renowned Water Lilies series, titled Nymphéas avec Reflets de Hautes Herbes, although official confirmation of the exact piece is still pending. The Monet sale forms part of a broader recovery exercise involving at least 12 artworks linked to the 1MDB scandal.

items.title: Penang says no to WFH for civil servants
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang government has decided not to adopt the proposed work-from-home (WFH) policy for its civil servants. Last week, Chow said the state government was weighing the possibility of adopting a WFH directive for civil servants following rising fuel costs linked to global conflict. He said the state would first study the federal government's directive before deciding how best to implement it locally.

items.title: New stricter RON95 and diesel rules take effect nationwide today
items.summary: KUCHING: Three new policies regulating the sale of RON95 petrol and diesel come into force today, marking the start of a stricter enforcement phase nationwide to curb subsidy leakages as well as fuel smuggling. Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry Enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said the measures were taken following an increase in demand and the growing risk of leakages, especially in border areas. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: High Court rejects Semantan Estate's bid for pre-Merdeka land records
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court today dismissed all discovery applications filed by Semantan Estate (1952) Sdn Bhd seeking pre-Merdeka land records for its compensation claim over the "Duta Enclave" land. Instead, the appellate court ordered that compensation be assessed by the High Court according to the land's 1956 market value, with appropriate adjustments, including interest. The Federal Court later dismissed Semantan's application for leave to appeal, effectively upholding the appellate court's ruling.

items.title: RON95 still being sold to foreign vehicles in Rantau Panjang despite ban
items.summary: RANTAU PANJANG: Several petrol stations here were found to still be selling RON95 to foreign-registered vehicles despite the enforcement of a nationwide ban beginning today. Checks at a number of stations near the border revealed that some vehicles, particularly motorcycles bearing Thai registration plates, were continuing to refuel with the subsidised petrol. "Some stations are strict and do not allow foreign vehicles to purchase RON95.

items.title: Macpma supports Miti's stance on aligning automotive investments with national objectives
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Automotive Component Parts Manufacturers (Macpma) supports the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry's (Miti) position that automotive investment incentives and manufacturing licences should be aligned with national development objectives. Macpma also expressed its full support for Miti's clarification on Malaysia's automotive investment policies, which are applied uniformly and consistently to all investors. "We acknowledge Miti's commitment to maintaining an open and welcoming investment environment while ensuring that new automotive investments contribute meaningfully to the long-term sustainability of the national automotive ecosystem.

items.title: Indonesian, 38, jailed for failing to pay fine for assaulting partner, 56
items.summary: Accused Sudirman, 38, pleaded guilty to both charges before magistrate Arun Noval Dass. In the first charge under Section 323 of the Penal Code, the father of three was accused of voluntarily causing hurt to his 56-year-old partner by repeatedly punching her in the face, causing injuries. Acting on information, police arrested the accused about 12pm on March 23.

items.title: Fatal crash on Penang Bridge sparks 7km traffic jam
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A fatal accident on the Penang Bridge caused severe traffic congestion stretching 7km during the morning peak today, leaving commuters stranded for hours. Early reports indicated the crash, believed to involve a car and a motorcycle, occurred on the bridge's mainland-bound carriageway, blocking at least two lanes. Traffic is congested over 7km.

items.title: ‘Mum, just recite Al-Fatihah for her’: Mother describes daughter’s grief and guilt after Zara Qairina’s death
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 1 — The latest child witness believed to be the last to have seen Zara Qairina Mahathir alive was feeling guilt and trauma from her encounter with Zara Qairina before she was found unconscious and died on July 16 last year. The 41-year-old mother of the child witness, who only came forward recently to give her witness account, told the Coroner’s Court that her child was emotional, distressed and started sobbing whenever recounting an incident involving the late Zara Qairina. “Nearly three months later, my child finally told me in more detail what she knew about the incident,” she said while reading her witness statement before coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan.

items.title: Energy Commission chief: Brace for rises in energy costs
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia must be prepared to face rising fuel costs for electricity generation following the current geopolitical situation in the Middle East. Energy Commission chief executive officer Siti Safinah Salleh said about 80 per cent of the country's natural gas cost for power generation is still fixed or tied to domestic gas. She said the remaining 20 per cent is imported, exposing the country to global market prices.

items.title: Widow relieved by murder charge, vows RM1m civil suit over fatal Klang crash
items.summary: KLANG, April 1 — The widow of Amirul Hafiz Omar expressed relief today after the driver accused of causing her husband’s death was charged with murder. Nor Nadia Abdul Majid, 32, said she was satisfied with the prosecution’s decision, Free Malaysia Today (FMT) reported. Technician R. Saktygaanapathy, 28, was charged with murdering Amirul, 33, at Jalan Raya Barat on March 29.

items.title: It’s going up, up, up: Malaysia records higher tourist arrivals in March despite Middle East turmoil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Malaysia’s overseas travellers increased last month despite the ongoing Middle East conflict, mainly boosted by visitors from the Asean and Asian regions, with countries such as China and Indonesia showing the strongest growth. Tourism Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing said Malaysia had 2,835,724 (2.8 million) international visitors for March 1 to March 26 this year, which is a 2.4 per cent increase compared to the 2,769,305 (over 2.76 million) for the same period last year. In terms of visitor arrivals to Malaysia, other countries with top growth percentage-wise are Indonesia (28.2 per cent) and Australia (22.2 per cent).

items.title: Thunderstorm warning issued for Cameron Highlands, Selangor, KL and seven other regions today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (METMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for multiple states across Peninsular Malaysia, Sabah, and Sarawak, effective until 4pm today The alert forecasts thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds for the affected areas. In Peninsular Malaysia, the warning covers the entire state of Perlis, parts of Kedah and Penang, most districts in Perak, and the Cameron Highlands in Pahang. Widespread storms are also expected across the majority of Selangor and WP Kuala Lumpur, several areas in Negeri Sembilan, and Alor Gajah in Melaka.

items.title: Police appeal for leads on two individuals missing since March 25 and 26 in Nibong Tebal
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL, April 1 — Police are actively trying to locate two individuals who have been reported missing after leaving their respective homes on March 25 and 26, and have not returned since. Seberang Perai Selatan police chief Supt Jay January Siowou said that the first individual is a 44-year-old man, Lim Hock Seng, who has been missing since March 25. “The other is a woman, Nur Ain Ariffen, 39, who has been reported missing since last week.

items.title: Higher Education Ministry urges industry to tap RM5m Public Private Research Network fund to boost productivity
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The Ministry of Higher Education Malaysia (MOHE) has called on industry players to take advantage of the RM5 million fund provided under the Public Private Research Network (PPRN) initiative to enhance company productivity. The ministry’s Deputy Secretary-General (Policy) Datuk Dr Megat Sany Megat Ahmad Supian said the initiative, which began in 2015, has recorded the implementation of more than 700 innovation projects involving collaborations between public and private universities and industry players. He said the initiative aims to cultivate knowledge transfer and expertise from researchers in higher education institutions (HEIs) through research collaborations with local companies that may lack expertise and capital.

items.title: Police seek public help to find missing 15‑year‑old girl in Mukah
items.summary: =MUKAH, April 1 — Police are appealing to the public to help locate a 15-year-old girl who went missing yesterday. Mukah police chief DSP Muhamad Rizal Alias said Angel Daina from Jalan Tiong Hua here was last seen wearing a pink short-sleeved T-shirt and long black pants. “Anyone who knows her whereabouts is advised to contact the nearest police station or the Mukah police station on 084-871222,” he added.

items.title: Malaysian vehicles among foreign cars required to have three‑quarters full tank to enter Brunei from April 1
items.summary: MIRI, April 1 — Malaysian vehicle owners are advised to fill before entering Brunei, as its government will require all foreign vehicles to have at least three-quarters of a tank of fuel starting April 1. “All foreign-registered vehicles entering Brunei Darussalam will be required to maintain a minimum fuel level of three-quarter tank upon entry. “Foreign vehicles that do not comply with this requirement will not be allowed to enter Brunei Darussalam,” the statement said.

items.title: Government withdraws appeal on Pastor Koh report; court awards RM15,000 costs to family
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 1 — The Malaysian government today withdrew its appeal against a High Court ruling, which had granted Pastor Raymond Koh’s family access to a classified report. While Nurul Farhana reportedly asked the court to not order the government to pay for legal costs, Koh's family's lawyer Michelle Wong had objected to this. On August 15, 2024, the High Court in Kuala Lumpur had ordered the police and government to give Koh's wife a special task force report on Amri's and Koh's disappearance.

items.title: Two teens drown during morning swim at Papar riverside campsite
items.summary: PAPAR, April 1 — A recreational swim at a riverside campsite here ended in tragedy when two teenagers drowned at Pokdi Campsite in Mandalipau here yesterday morning. Papar Fire and Rescue (Bomba) Station chief Asst Fire Supt Rozlan Osman said firefighters were dispatched to the location after receiving a distress call at 10.10am. He said members of the public managed to pull both victims from the river before firefighters arrived at the scene.
